title: World-class UX-for-data-dashboards review of the just-shipped dashboard tabs
brief:
PERSONA: Act as a world-class UX designer specializing in data-dense operator dashboards (think: Stripe, Linear, Vercel, Retool, Posthog, Looker — the tier where information density and scannability matter, but novice operators still need to find their way). Visual taste tier: best in industry. Light theme is mandatory. Plain Tailwind only — no shadcn (not installed).

The casesdaily admin dashboard at `/admin/dashboard/*` was just polished with row color coding, dynamic orders fill, profitability margin columns, and updated meta KPIs. The user said: 'It's just FUGLY UX. I want you to over-write your innate bias to using your own agents, and use the gemini agents here to do a number of quick calls and changes... look at the design of the expansion of showing the data.'

Produce a TARGETED design critique + concrete diffs for two of the most-used surfaces, focused on:

(A) **LeadsTable expanded row** — currently a flat 3-column grid of fields + textareas. Re-architect into a clean two-pane layout: left = identity / source / attribution (compact key-value list with copy buttons); right = editable notes & overrides. Reduce visual noise. Add subtle dividers, consistent label typography, copy-to-clipboard affordances, and a small action bar at the top of the expansion (Open Lead Detail, Open Attorney, View Landing Page, Open in GHL). Keep all existing fields and handlers; only restructure layout + typography + spacing.

(B) **Profitability tab** — current headline grid has too many cards of identical visual weight. Establish a clear visual hierarchy: 2-3 hero metrics at top (Net Profit, ROAS, Cost/Billed) in a bigger format with sparkline-ready space; remaining metrics in a denser secondary strip below. Per-buyer table: zebra striping, sticky header on scroll, right-align all numerics with tabular-nums, soft column dividers, and clear separation of the 'Internal' pinned row (currently bg-blue-50/30 — keep but improve contrast).

Output must be full-file rewrites of:
- casesdaily/src/components/admin/dashboard/LeadsTable.tsx (expansion section only — keep rest IDENTICAL)
- casesdaily/src/app/admin/dashboard/profitability/page.tsx (full file rewrite)

For LeadsTable, do NOT touch the row-tinting logic introduced earlier; build on top of it.
outputContract:
Markdown:
1. **Critique** (8–15 lines, no more) — what's wrong with the current UX, the principles applied to the fix.
2. **Files** — full fenced rewrites:
   ```typescript
   // FILE: casesdaily/src/components/admin/dashboard/LeadsTable.tsx
   <full file>
   ```
   ```typescript
   // FILE: casesdaily/src/app/admin/dashboard/profitability/page.tsx
   <full file>
   ```
3. **What the user will notice** — 5 bullets.

Strict rules: Light theme. Plain Tailwind. No new dependencies. Keep ALL existing handlers, props, server data shapes. Do not lose any field or action.
